description: Enhance multi-tab detection to differentiate between same-project and same-workspace collisions, and update the warning UI with specific messages per the user requirements.
steps: Step 1 - Add new state variables in src/App.jsx near line 523 (alongside existing isMultiTab state): Add `const [isMultiTabSameWorkspace, setIsMultiTabSameWorkspace] = useState(false);` for workspace-level detection.

Step 2 - Generate a unique tab ID. Add a useRef near line 527: `const tabIdRef = useRef(Date.now().toString(36) + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2));` This identifies the current tab uniquely so it can ignore its own messages.

Step 3 - Rewrite the BroadcastChannel useEffect (lines 1083-1165) to include project and workspace info in messages. The heartbeat message should be: `{ type: 'presence', tabId, projectId: activeProjectId, workspaceId: activeTab }`. The leave message should be: `{ type: 'leave', tabId }`. On receiving a presence message: (a) ignore messages from own tabId, (b) track other tabs in a Map ref keyed by tabId with value { projectId, workspaceId, lastSeen }. (c) derive isMultiTab = any other tab has the same projectId. (d) derive isMultiTabSameWorkspace = any other tab has same projectId AND same workspaceId. Use a cleanup interval that removes entries older than 10 seconds. Add activeProjectId and activeTab to the useEffect dependency array so the heartbeat updates when the user switches project or workspace.
Step 4 - Handle the leave message: remove the tab from the tracking Map and re-derive both flags.
Step 5 - For the localStorage fallback (catch block), extend the stored object to include projectId and workspaceId, and apply the same derivation logic.
Step 6 - Update the existing useEffect that clears showMultiTabTooltip (around line 1167) to also clear the tooltip when neither flag is true.

Step 7 - Replace the warning UI section (lines 5195-5225) with a new block that shows differentiated warnings: (a) When isMultiTabSameWorkspace is true, show BOTH a project-level warning AND a workspace-level warning (as separate elements or a combined tooltip). The project warning text: '\u26a0 This project is already open in another tab. Task data may be overwritten.' The workspace warning text: '\u26a0 This workspace is already open in another tab. Changes may overwrite each other.' (b) When isMultiTab is true but isMultiTabSameWorkspace is false, show only the project-level warning. (c) Keep the existing amber styling pattern (amber-50 bg, amber-200 border, AlertTriangle icon from lucide-react). Show the full warning text on sm+ screens and icon-only on xs screens.

Step 8 - Ensure cleanup on beforeunload sends the leave message with tabId, and when the useEffect re-runs (due to activeProjectId/activeTab change), it sends a new presence immediately so other tabs update their tracking.
acceptance_criteria: The app builds successfully with npm run build (no compile errors).
The BroadcastChannel messages include projectId and workspaceId fields.
A unique tabId is generated per tab and included in messages.
isMultiTab is true when another tab has the same activeProjectId.
isMultiTabSameWorkspace is true when another tab has the same activeProjectId AND same activeTab (workspace).
When only project matches (different workspaces), the UI shows: 'This project is already open in another tab. Task data may be overwritten.' with warning emoji.
When both project and workspace match, the UI shows both the project-level warning AND the workspace-level warning: 'This workspace is already open in another tab. Changes may overwrite each other.'
When the user switches workspace in one tab, the other tab's flags update within one heartbeat cycle (4 seconds).
On tab close (beforeunload), a leave message is sent so other tabs clear warnings promptly.
No synchronization, merge logic, OT, CRDTs, lock server, or collaboration features are introduced.
verification: Run `cd /projects/sandbox/ClearMind && npm install && npm run build` and confirm it completes with exit code 0.
findings: Build succeeds cleanly. No test suite to run. Post-review fixes applied: (1) otherTabsRef.current.clear() at top of useEffect body to prevent stale entries from previous context, (2) deriveFlags() debounced by 200ms to prevent warning flash when sibling tab switches workspace, (3) deriveFlagsImmediate() called after Map clear and after sendPresence() to ensure flags are in a known state. Cleanup functions also clear the debounce timer. CRITICAL BUG FIX (e543ac6): The effect crashed the app with a white screen (TypeError: W0 is not a constructor) because it fired with empty activeProjectId/activeTab on mount, then re-fired rapidly when values were set. postMessage on a closed channel during cleanup threw InvalidStateError. Fixed by: (a) early return guard when values are empty, (b) closed flag + safePostMessage wrapper with try-catch, (c) try-catch around cleanup leave message. Temporary console.log diagnostics added for channel lifecycle.
